metadata.title: Important Days and Events Current Affairs MCQs

questions.question: World Unani Day is celebrated globally every year on which day?
questions.explanation: World Unani Day is observed annually on February 11th to commemorate the birth anniversary of Hakim Ajmal Khan, an eminent Indian Unani physician, scholar, and social reformer. The theme for World Unani Day 2026 is “Innovation and Evidence in Unani Practice,” highlighting the integration of classical Unani wisdom with modern scientific research. The Ministry of Ayush, through the Central Council for Research in Unani Medicine (CCRUM), is organizing a two-day national conference in Mumbai on February 14–15, 2026, in hybrid mode.

questions.question: The 17th Tribal Youth Exchange Programme is a flagship initiative of which ministry?

options.label: B
options.text: Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports

options.label: C
options.text: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment

answer.label: B
answer.text: Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports

questions.explanation: The 17th Tribal Youth Exchange Programme was held in Kancheepuram district of Tamil Nadu. It is a flagship initiative of the Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports in coordination with the Ministry of Home Affairs. Around 200 tribal youth from Left Wing Extremism-affected districts of Odisha, Madhya Pradesh, Jharkhand and Maharashtra participated in the event. The week-long camp promotes national integration and cultural understanding.

questions.question: Which state hosted first Soybean Festival to boost farmer empowerment?
questions.explanation: Nagaland hosted first Soybean Festival at Agri Expo, Chümoukedima on 20–21 February with more than 200 farmers, scientists and officials. Event aimed to boost soybean cultivation and farmer empowerment. Experts from Nagaland University and Indian Council of Agricultural Research–National Bureau of Plant Genetic Resources (ICAR-NBPGR), New Delhi promoted productivity and sustainable practices. Nagaland’s average yield is 12.6 quintals per hectare. It is higher than national average of 11.79 quintals per hectare, showing strong growth potential.

questions.question: Which ministry organized ‘Him-CONNECT’ as part of the The Energy and Resources Institute’s World Sustainable Development Summit (WSDS)?

options.label: B
options.text: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change

answer.label: B
answer.text: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change

questions.explanation: The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change is organising ‘Him-CONNECT’ from 25–27 February 2026 in New Delhi as part of the The Energy and Resources Institute’s World Sustainable Development Summit (WSDS). Him-CONNECT is designed as a collaborative platform to connect researchers working in the Indian Himalayan Region (IHR) with start-ups, investors and policymakers to facilitate the scaling and on-ground application of research outcomes. The platform will showcase more than 24 technologies, prototypes, patents and pilot projects developed under the Ministry’s National Mission on Himalayan Studies (NMHS).

questions.question: International Polar Bear Day is observed every year on which day?
questions.explanation: International Polar Bear Day is observed every year on 27 February to spread awareness about threats from climate change and melting Arctic sea ice. It is organised by Polar Bears International. The day highlights the vulnerable denning period when mother polar bears and cubs stay inside snow dens. Polar bears depend on sea ice to hunt seals for survival. Rising temperatures reduce sea ice, shrinking hunting grounds and lowering cub survival rates. The day promotes education, scientific research and conservation of Arctic ecosystems.

questions.question: Where was the 4th edition of Tulip Festival 2026 organized?
questions.explanation: New Delhi Municipal Council organised the 4th edition of Tulip Festival 2026 at Shanti Path, New Delhi. The festival features nearly 5.5 lakh tulips and is held in collaboration with the Netherlands to strengthen bilateral ties. Indigenous tulips developed by Council of Scientific and Industrial Research bloom during Delhi’s short winter and spring period. The initiative promotes local bulb cultivation, aligns with the “Viksit Bharat @2047” vision, and serves as a soft-power diplomatic tool.

questions.question: Where was the inaugural edition of the Raisina Science Diplomacy Initiative (SDI) held in March 2026?
questions.explanation: The inaugural edition of the Raisina Science Diplomacy Initiative (SDI) was held on 5 March 2026 at Bharat Mandapam, New Delhi. It was organised as part of the Raisina Dialogue. The initiative was jointly launched by Office of the Principal Scientific Adviser to the Government of India and Observer Research Foundation (ORF). It aims to create a global platform focused on the growing role of science and technology in international diplomacy and policy-making.

questions.question: No Smoking Day 2026 is observed on which day?
questions.explanation: No Smoking Day 2026 is observed on 11 March 2026 to encourage people to quit tobacco and adopt healthier lifestyles. The campaign spreads awareness about the harmful effects of smoking and supports people overcoming nicotine addiction. It was first launched in 1984 in the United Kingdom and is now observed every year on the second Wednesday of March. Governments and health organisations use the day to promote tobacco cessation programmes and public health awareness campaigns.

questions.question: Which ministry launched Bharat Tex 2026, India’s flagship global textile event?

options.label: C
options.text: Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises

questions.explanation: Ministry of Textiles launched Bharat Tex 2026, India’s flagship global textile event, showcasing the entire textile value chain from fibres and yarns to fabrics, garments, technical textiles, and sustainable innovations. The event will be held 14-17 July 2026 at Bharat Mandapam, New Delhi, building on the success of 2024 and 2025 editions. Bharat Tex 2026 aims to strengthen India’s position as a reliable sourcing and investment destination, enable strategic partnerships, and support policy reforms for textile growth.

questions.question: Where was Yoga Mahotsav 2026 held?
questions.explanation: Yoga Mahotsav 2026 was inaugurated in New Delhi by Prataprao Jadhav, marking the 100-day countdown to the 12th International Day of Yoga (IDY) 2026. The event launched “10 Yoga Protocols for Non-Communicable Diseases (NCDs)”, where NCDs (Non-Communicable Diseases) include lifestyle illnesses such as diabetes, hypertension, and heart disease. The programme is organised by the Ministry of Ayush, the nodal ministry responsible for promoting IDY globally. A special “Yoga for Air Travel” protocol was introduced, consisting of a 5-minute seated routine of yogic postures, breathing exercises, and short meditation to reduce travel stress and improve blood circulation during flights.
